artifacts.claimText: NTUT Academic Affairs guidance says instructors may use generative AI as an auxiliary tool for teaching design and materials preparation, but should carefully review generated content for credibility, correctness, bias, and academic fairness.

artifacts.claimText: NTUT guidance advises students not to over-rely on AI tools, to evaluate AI output, to label AI-generated results when used, and not to submit generated output directly as their own reports or assignments.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 因應生成式AI工具之教學參考指引(112.06.26更新)
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 指導學生正確運用AI工具，協助課業及自我學習，但不宜過度依賴；使用時應思考與評估判斷其內容的正確性及合理性，如使用AI生成結果建議宜標註，並經思考後提出個人觀點，不宜將生成結果直接作為自身的報告或作業。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students should be guided to use AI tools properly, avoid over-reliance, evaluate correctness and reasonableness, label AI-generated results when used, and not submit generated output directly as their own work.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.title: National Taipei University of Technology Generative AI Teaching Guidance
artifacts.summary: Official NTUT Office of Academic Affairs sources provide Traditional Chinese teaching reference guidance for generative AI tools. The evidence supports guidance-scoped claims on instructor review of AI-generated materials, course-level AI-use rules in syllabi, student disclosure and responsibility, and privacy/security cautions. No central binding university-wide AI policy was found in the checked official sources.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are scoped to official Academic Affairs teaching reference guidance and should not be read as a central binding university-wide AI policy.
Official news and implementation-plan documents were reviewed but rejected for claim extraction because they were not stable policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
